edible_parts: Flower nectar (nibble, minor); not the invasive kind.
other_uses: Well-behaved native climbing honeysuckle with clusters of fragrant yellow tubular flowers.
prep: A native, non-invasive honeysuckle grown as a hummingbird vine; children sip the flower nectar. Berries are for birds. Choose over invasive Japanese honeysuckle.
soil: Medium, well-drained rocky soils; woodland edges.
risks: Berries not for eating; nectar sip only.
buy: Native-plant nurseries and native seed suppliers.
